overview: This week I built a full evaluation pipeline for the SAFe Feature Spec System, turning prompt engineering from guesswork into measurable, data-driven iteration. The system includes a SQLite backend with token instrumentation across six agents, a 6-case golden set with bare/boosted variants, a five-tab Streamlit dashboard, and an AI-powered improvement suggester powered by Claude Sonnet. My first A/B test produced a data-driven Router v2 promotion that eliminated an intermittent EXPERIENCE misclassification bug.

keyPoints.title: SQLite pipeline with SHA-256 content-addressing stores all prompt versions idempotently
keyPoints.description: Claude Code designed the content-addressed hashing system so registering the same prompt twice returns the same ID rather than creating a duplicate — the same idempotency principle I encountered with Pinecone upserts in Week 7.

keyPoints.title: V1 baseline: 5/6 passed, $1.56 total, EXPERIENCE routing failed intermittently
keyPoints.description: Both EXPERIENCE cases misrouted to CAPABILITY in the baseline run, making a previously hidden Week 8 bug visible in data for the first time.

keyPoints.title: Router A/B test revealed v1 flakiness; v2 achieved consistent 6/6 correct classifications
keyPoints.description: Running two rounds of 12 Haiku calls at roughly half a cent total showed v1 misfired on EXPERIENCE in one run but passed in another — intermittent failures that a single test would have missed entirely.

keyPoints.title: LLM-as-Judge scores varied ±7 points across identical runs at temperature 0.0
keyPoints.description: Running the same evaluation three times with identical inputs and prompts produced scores of 71, 78, and 71 — proving that a single evaluation score is a data point, not a verdict.
keyPoints.sectionName: LLM-as-Judge Variance Is Real (Even at Temperature 0.0)

keyPoints.title: Bare-to-boosted score spreads of +14 to +25 points quantify the value of PM context
keyPoints.description: The consistent gap between minimal bare inputs and PM-enriched boosted inputs across all three feature types gave me a concrete, data-backed way to measure how much stakeholder context improves output quality.
keyPoints.sectionName: The Bare/Boosted Spread Quantifies the Value of PM Input

keyPoints.title: Improvement Suggester proposes specific prompt edits with diffs but never auto-applies them
keyPoints.description: Claude Sonnet at temperature 0.2 analyzes aggregated section scores and reviewer feedback to generate quoted, diagnosed, and rationale-backed suggestions — keeping the human-in-the-loop pattern from Week 8 applied to prompt optimization.
